changed_files.patch:
@@ -201,7 +201,7 @@ jobs:
     strategy:
       fail-fast: false
       matrix:
-        python-version: ["3.11", "3.12", "3.13", "3.14"]
+        python-version: ["3.11", "3.12"]
 
     steps:
       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
@@ -217,6 +217,7 @@ jobs:
         run: |
           python -m venv .venv
           echo "$PWD/.venv/bin" >> $GITHUB_PATH
+          echo "PYTHONPATH=$PWD/python:$PWD/mlir" >> $GITHUB_ENV
 
       - name: Mark GitHub Actions workdir as safe
         run: git config --global --add safe.directory "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE"
@@ -237,12 +238,13 @@ jobs:
             -DCMAKE_C_COMPILER=clang-19 \
             -DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER=clang++-19 \
             -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug \
+            -DPYTHON_BUILD_FRONTEND=ON \
+            -Dpybind11_DIR=$GITHUB_WORKSPACE/.venv/lib/python${{ matrix.python-version }}/site-packages/pybind11/share/cmake/pybind11 \
             -DMLIR_BUILD_FRONTEND=ON \
+            -DMLIR_BUILD_BINDINGS=ON \
             -DMLIR_BUILD_TESTS=ON \
             -DMLIR_DIR=/usr/lib/llvm-19/lib/cmake/mlir \
             -DLLVM_EXTERNAL_LIT=/usr/lib/llvm-19/build/utils/lit/lit.py \
-            -DWITH_SYMENGINE_THREAD_SAFE=ON \
-            -DWITH_SYMENGINE_RCP=ON \
             -DINSTALL_GTEST=OFF \
             -DBUILD_TESTS:BOOL=OFF \
             -DBUILD_BENCHMARKS:BOOL=OFF \
@@ -255,14 +257,6 @@ jobs:
           cd build/
           ninja check-docc-mlir
 
-      - name: Build Python bindings
-        run: |
-          # Install docc-compiler package first
-          pip install -Ccmake.build-type=Debug -e python/
-
-          # Then build mlir bindings
-          pip install -Ccmake.build-type=Debug --no-build-isolation -e mlir/
-
       - name: Integration Tests
         if: matrix.python-version == '3.11' || matrix.python-version == '3.12'
         run: |

@@ -45,5 +45,12 @@ target_include_directories(sdfg-mlir PRIVATE
     ${PROJECT_BINARY_DIR}/include
 )
 
+add_custom_command(TARGET sdfg-mlir POST_BUILD
+        COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E create_symlink
+        "$<TARGET_FILE:sdfg-mlir>"
+        "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/../docc/mlir/$<TARGET_FILE_NAME:sdfg-mlir>"
+        COMMENT "Creating symlink to docc.mlir._sdfg_mlir in src dir"
+)
+
 # Install the Python module to the docc/mlir package directory
 install(TARGETS sdfg-mlir DESTINATION docc/mlir COMPONENT docc_mlir)

@@ -26,12 +26,20 @@ set_target_properties(sdfg-py PROPERTIES
     CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON
     OUTPUT_NAME "_sdfg"
 )
+
+add_custom_command(TARGET sdfg-py POST_BUILD
+        COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E create_symlink
+        "$<TARGET_FILE:sdfg-py>"
+        "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/docc/sdfg/$<TARGET_FILE_NAME:sdfg-py>"
+        COMMENT "Creating symlink to docc.sdfg._sdfg in src dir"
+)
+
 target_include_directories(sdfg-py
     PUBLIC
         $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/bindings>
 )
 
-target_link_libraries(sdfg-py PRIVATE sdfg::sdfg sdfg::opt sdfg::rpc nlohmann_json::nlohmann_json ${CMAKE_DL_LIBS})
+target_link_libraries(sdfg-py PRIVATE sdfg::sdfg sdfg::opt sdfg::rpc docc::c-compile nlohmann_json::nlohmann_json ${CMAKE_DL_LIBS})
 
 if(TARGET docc::target::et)
     target_link_libraries(sdfg-py PRIVATE docc::target::et)

commit_summaries.message:
Symlink our python native bindings into a common folder structure matching the packages

Allows using the native bindings build once with cmake with the python & mlir test suites. Towards considerably saving on compile time, because each python lib no longer needs to recompile the entire cmake project independently. But tests using that will be further away from the pip-install variant.

Symlink python native bindings into the source folders & support finding internally needed paths in-place as well

 * moved runtime lib-path discovery into c-compile library and detect pip install vs. in-place / from git source directory runs and set the paths to our headers and libraries accordingly
Fix: did not use correct paths-mode for standalone-docc in-place operation
Updated mlir tests to use in-place build
Move PYTHONPATH setting into a step, to have access to the proper paths
